name: wildfire-exposure-eo validation AOI — Serra de Monchique (Algarve WUI)

properties.name: Serra de Monchique / Silves (2018 Algarve WUI fire)
properties.description:
Validation AOI centered on the August 2018 Serra de Monchique fire (Algarve), which burned approximately 27,200 ha and threatened the town of Monchique — the primary wildland-urban interface case study in southern Portugal. Copernicus EMS activation EMSR303 provides independent perimeter reference. This AOI is authored new (not promoted from an alt file); the bbox is a best-effort working value derived from the operationalization.md DRAFT (docs/operationalization.md §5b: '[-8.72, 37.18, -8.38, 37.45]') and expanded slightly south and west to be clearly larger than the pilot (~1774 km2 vs pilot ~902 km2). Different climate zone (Mediterranean Algarve) and WUI character from the other three AOIs.

# TODO(provenance): Verify final bbox against EMSR303 delineation (https://emergency.copernicus.eu/mapping/list-of-components/EMSR303) and ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2018 annual release before freezing. Current coordinates are best-effort estimates anchored on Serra de Monchique (37.32°N, 8.54°W) and the documented fire extent. Do NOT treat as final without EMSR303 or ICNF boundary verification. Source for draft bbox: docs/operationalization.md §5b (2026-06-16).
properties.rationale: The WUI axis missing from the other three AOIs: a fire that explicitly threatened a populated urban centre (Monchique town, ~5,000 residents). Different climate zone (Mediterranean Algarve) exercises the score on a completely different fire-weather driver and fuel mix (thermophilous shrub, eucalyptus, cork-oak). The 2018 season was the second largest burned area in Algarve since records began. This AOI completes the four-AOI stress-test matrix: historical scar scale (Pedrógão), protected-area/high-mountain (Estrela + Gerês), WUI/urban-threat (Monchique).
properties.source: Bbox derived from docs/operationalization.md §5b DRAFT working value, expanded to be larger than pilot. Fire event source: ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2018 (https://www.icnf.pt/florestas/gfr/gfrint/ifiarf); Copernicus EMS EMSR303 (https://emergency.copernicus.eu/mapping/list-of-components/EMSR303). Both accessed 2026-06-16. NOTE: bbox coordinates are unverified against EMSR303 or ICNF 2018 perimeters — see TODO(provenance) above.
properties.anchor_fire_source: ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2018 — Serra de Monchique; ~27,200 ha per ICNF annual report. Source: https://www.icnf.pt/florestas/gfr/gfrint/ifiarf (accessed 2026-06-16). NOTE: figure cited from ICNF only (non-negotiable #(c) — do not average with EFFIS which uses a different methodology).
properties.copernicus_ems: EMSR303 — independent perimeter reference; verify against https://emergency.copernicus.eu/mapping/list-of-components/EMSR303
properties.icnf_coverage: strong — ICNF 2018 perimeters are finalised and in the canonical annual release
properties.osm_density: moderate — Monchique town and Silves city provide infrastructure; verify before relying on asset counts
properties.validation_notes: BBOX IS DRAFT — see TODO(provenance). Must be verified against EMSR303 extent and ICNF 2018 Áreas Ardidas before Phase B. The smoke tile (smoke_monchique.geojson) is centred on Monchique town (~37.322°N, 8.540°W) as the highest-density infrastructure sub-tile, also unverified against actual ICNF burn intersection. Phase A gate (b) — ICNF intersection — must be confirmed once the bbox is finalised.
properties.status: DRAFT — bbox unverified against EMSR303 / ICNF 2018; see TODO(provenance)
